meaning: adj.效率低的,效率差的,(指人)不能胜任的,无能的
examples: The whole process is wasteful and inefficient.
The train company is overstaffed and notoriously inefficient.
Overall, barter is a very inefficient means of organizing transactions.
The tariffs allow even more inefficient firms to gather a profit in the protected industry.
"They inherited a pretty inefficient business from ibm" and are still cleaning it up, Spooner says.
